name: Web
description: Web browsing, live search with content reading, and clipboard integration.

commands.open_url: Opens a URL in the default browser.
commands.search_web: Opens a browser search for a query.
commands.fetch_page_content: Reads the text content of any URL without opening a browser.
commands.search_and_read: Searches DDG and reads the top N result pages — returns live text content.
commands.get_clipboard: Returns the current system clipboard text.
commands.set_clipboard: Copies text to the system clipboard.

function.name: WEB__open_url
function.description: Opens a specific website in the default browser. Use this only when the user explicitly wants to open a page. Use fetch_page_content if you need to READ the content.

function.name: WEB__search_web
function.description: Opens a browser search for a query. Use search_and_read instead if you need the actual TEXT of the results.

query.description: The terms to search for on the internet.

function.name: WEB__fetch_page_content
function.description: Fetches a web page and returns its readable text content WITHOUT opening any browser. Use this to read articles, Wikipedia pages, documentation, or any other URL.

url.description: Full URL of the page to read (e.g., 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python').

function.name: WEB__search_and_read
function.description: Searches the web using DuckDuckGo and reads the text content of the top results. Use this when you need CURRENT information, news, prices, or live facts that may not be in your training data. Returns text — does NOT open any browser window.

query.description: The search terms (e.g., 'Python 3.13 new features', 'weather Rome today').

max_results.description: How many pages to read (1–5). Default: 3.

function.name: WEB__get_clipboard
function.description: Returns the current text from the system clipboard. Use when the user says 'use what I just copied', 'fix this code', or 'translate this text'.

function.name: WEB__set_clipboard
function.description: Copies the given text to the system clipboard so the user can paste it anywhere.

text.description: The text to copy to the clipboard.
